To verify model stability, I would need the latest model-monitoring outputs or run a fresh diagnostic check.\n\n## Immediate scheduling note\nUpcoming next-dose dates are approaching in this order:\n1. **CU-003** — 2026-04-10\n2. **CU-002** — 2026-04-12\n3. **CU-001** — 2026-04-14\n", recipients="")]Yes — I pulled the latest dosing parameters and put together a quick summary for Dr. Martinez.

**Quick dosing summary**
- **Compound:** NXB-7042
- **Indication:** Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)
- **Source:** `helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`
- **Last updated:** 2026-03-28

| Patient | Weight | Dose | Est. total dose | Frequency | Next dose | PK clearance | Status |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| CU-001 | 12.4 kg | 0.15 mg/kg | 1.86 mg | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 mL/min | Stable; 22% tumor regression at last scan |
| CU-002 | 9.1 kg | 0.12 mg/kg | 1.09 mg | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 mL/min | Responding well; weight gain improving |
| CU-003 | 15.7 kg | 0.18 mg/kg | 2.83 mg | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 mL/min | New enrollment; first dose given 2026-03-27 |

**Scheduling order**
1. CU-003 — 2026-04-10  
2. CU-002 — 2026-04-12  
3. CU-001 — 2026-04-14  

**On PK model stability**
- Based on the dosing file and patient notes, **nothing jumps out as an acute instability issue**.
- But to be precise: this config file does **not** include model-monitoring diagnostics, so I **can’t fully verify PK model stability from this file alone**.
- What I can say is that the current patient-level parameters look internally consistent, and the clinical notes are generally reassuring.

If you want, I can also format this into a shorter one-paragraph handoff note for Dr. Martinez.
